steps.title: Identify the clause structure
steps.content: The blank follows the singular subject “it” and the adverb “regularly” and precedes the direct object “all its needles,” making it the main verb of an independent clause.

steps.title: Apply subject‑verb agreement
steps.content:
The verb must agree with the singular subject “it” in the present simple, so it takes the –s ending: sheds.
The other three either lack the –s, use a past participle, or a gerund, violating subject‑verb agreement.
That matches choice C — C is correct.
